LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ROEBBELEN CONTRACTING, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT NEGLIGENTLY CONSTRUCTED THE RAMP AND/OR CONTROLLED THE PREMISES AT THE TIME OF CONSTRUCTION. DEFENDANT'S REMAINING ARGUMENTS LACK MERIT. DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENTS REGARDING THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS RAISE FACTUAL ISSUES THAT CANNOT BE RESOLVED ON DEMURRER. COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL DOES NOT APPLY TO THE DISCRIMINATION CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE THE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION RULING IS NOT A FINAL JUDGMENT ON THE MERITS. LAW OF THE CASE DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE THERE WAS NEVER AN APPELLATE OPINION. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT BY EMAIL TODAY. The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROEBBELEN CONTRACTING, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action and overruled as to the second cause of action. Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege that defendant negligently constructed the ramp and/or controlled the premises at the time of construction. Defendant's remaining arguments lack merit. Defendant's arguments regarding the statute of limitations raise factual issues that cannot be resolved on demurrer. Collateral estoppel does not apply to the discrimination cause of action because the summary adjudication ruling is not a final judgment on the merits. Law of the case does not apply because there was never an appellate opinion. Plaintiff's counsel to submit a proposed order to the court by email today.
